zh.Southampton, Hampshire, England, United: 英國英格蘭哈普爾素坦普頓
zh.Avon Tyrrell, Hampshire, England: 英國英格蘭哈普爾阿沃
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell於1165年出生在英國哈普爾阿沃時，他的父親Sir Roger Avon Tyrell20歲，母親Isabel de Vignacourt15歲。他於1195年與Lady Marie Senarpont結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1210年在他的家鄉享年46歲。

zh-hans.Southampton, Hampshire, England, United: 英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓
zh-hans.Avon Tyrrell, Hampshire, England: 英国英格兰哈普爾阿沃
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell于1165年出生在英国哈普爾阿沃时，他的父亲Sir Roger Avon Tyrell20岁，母亲Isabel de Vignacourt15岁。他于1195年与Lady Marie Senarpont结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1210年在他的家乡享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Tyrrell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): perhaps a nickname from a derivative of Old French tirer ‘to pull’, in the same sense as French Tirand ‘one who pulls on the reins’, hence ‘obstinate, stubborn’. This name may have become confused with Turrell . A branch of this Anglo-Norman family has been in Ireland since the 12th century. Its name has been Gaelicized as Tirial.
English: habitational name from Tirril (Cumbria), which derives from Old Norse tyri ‘resinous wood’ + erg ‘shieling’.

variants.name: Terrel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Terrell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Tirrell
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Terral
meanings: Altered form of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Terrill
meanings: English (Cornwall; of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Turrell
meanings: English (southeastern and East Anglia):

most frequently an Anglo-Norman French pronunciation of Thorold (Old Norse Thóraldr, from the elements Thórr ‘Thor’ + valdr ‘power, rule’). This name and that in 2 may have become confused with Tyrrell .
from Middle English T(h)urild (Old Norse Thórhildr, Thorild, the latter a rare woman's name from the divine name Thórr ‘Thor’ + hildr ‘battle’).

variants.name: Tyrell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Trill
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived by the turnstile at the entrance to a village.
English (Sussex, also Kent): nickname, perhaps from Middle English tril(le) ‘turn, roll, whirl, spin’ (origin unknown, but perhaps Old English tyrl).

English (Sussex, also Kent) and Welsh: habitational name from one or more places named with Middle English tril(le) ‘turn, roll, whirl, spin’ (Old English tyrl), often used to describe moving water, such as Trill in Axminster (Devon), Tirle Brook (Gloucestershire and Worcestershire), and Trill Mill Brook (Oxfordshire).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

nameMeanings.name: Edmond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Edmund  , now in quite frequent use in the English-speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell was born in 1165, in Avon, Hampshire, England, his father, Sir Roger Avon Tyrell, was 20 and his mother, Isabel de Vignacourt, was 15. He married Lady Marie Senarpont in 1195. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1210, in his hometown, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell est né en 1165, à Avon, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger Avon Tyrell, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Vignacourt, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Lady Marie Senarpont en 1195. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1210, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell 1165 in Avon, Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Roger Avon Tyrell 20 und seine Mutter Isabel de Vignacourt 15. Er hat 1195 Lady Marie Senarpont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1210 im Alter von 46 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell è nato il nel 1165 nel Avon, Hampshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Roger Avon Tyrell, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Isabel de Vignacourt, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Lady Marie Senarpont nel 1195. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1210, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 46 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrellが1165年にイギリスハンプシュアイアアボンで生まれた時，父Sir Roger Avon Tyrellは20歳，母Isabel de Vignacourtは15歳であった。1195年にLady Marie Senarpontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は約1210年に故郷で46歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell(이)가 1165년 영국 함프실르 아폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Roger Avon Tyrell(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel de Vignacourt(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1195년 Lady Marie Senarpont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1210년 그의 고향에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell nasceu em 1165, em Avon, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Roger Avon Tyrell, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Isabel de Vignacourt, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Marie Senarpont em 1195. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1210, em sua cidade natal, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell родился в 1165 г. в местности авон, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Roger Avon Tyrell было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Vignacourt, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Marie Senarpont в 1195 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1210 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edward Edmond Tyrrell nació en 1165, en Avon, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Roger Avon Tyrell, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Isabel de Vignacourt, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Lady Marie Senarpont en 1195. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1210, en su pueblo, a la edad de 46 años.
